business.headline: Even the bank didn't help me: What victims face after reporting digital fraudFor part two of our series on Banking Risks in Digital India, I spoke to victims of digital fraud to understand what they experienced after being scammed. Their stories show how the aftermath can be just as distressing as the fraud itself.

Commercial LPG up by nearly Rs 1,000: Why have prices increased?Commercial LPG cylinder prices have risen again as global oil tensions keep costs elevated. The move shields households for now but raises pressure on restaurants, hotels and small businesses.

Maharashtra Day on May 1: Is the stock market open or closed today?The BSE and NSE are closed on May 1 for Maharashtra Day across key trading segments. Commodity derivatives will reopen in the evening, while traders should account for settlement and liquidity effects.

Commercial, mini LPG prices hiked by Rs 993 and Rs 261, no change in domestic ratesCommercial LPG cylinder prices have surged by Rs 993 to Rs 3,071.50 in Delhi, while domestic LPG, petrol, diesel, and kerosene rates remain unchanged to shield consumers.

Bajaj Finance raises FD rates up to 7.40%: Should you lock in now?The company has increased rates by up to 45 basis points, with the biggest jump seen in longer tenures. This means investors looking to park money for a few years could now earn better returns.

Markets end lower as crude stays high, rupee hits record lowMarkets closed lower on Thursday as high crude prices and a weak rupee weighed on sentiment. The slide highlighted persistent inflation and capital flow concerns, even as IT and pharma offered limited support.

Bajaj Finserv announces dividend, reports strong FY26 growthBajaj Finserv reported record FY26 income and profit and announced a special dividend with its Q4 results. The update also marked the completion of Allianz's stake exit, leaving its insurance businesses fully Bajaj-owned.

India-NZ FTA opens doors, but only prepared firms will benefitSiddharth Shankar says the India-New Zealand FTA offers big openings for trade and talent. He argues only firms with strong systems, compliance and execution will truly benefit.

8th Pay Commission: What worker bodies have demanded ahead of salary reviewWorker representatives laid out pay, pension and service demands in the first 8th Pay Commission meeting in Delhi. The staff side said the talks ended positively but pressed for wider consultation and more meetings.

OnEMI Technology IPO: Weak Day 1 response, is it risky for long-term investors?Kissht IPO was subscribed just 0.25 times on Day 1, indicating a slow start. The retail portion saw particularly weak interest, with subscriptions at only 0.06 times.

Where should a 25-year-old invest to build wealth: SIPs, stocks or crypto?At 25, with time on your side, what's the smartest way to invest, i.e., SIPs (Systematic Investment Plans), stocks or crypto? Let's have a look.
Big EPFO update: Minimum pension may rise 7.5 times, ATM withdrawals soonThe government is mulling a sharp increase in the minimum pension under the Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS-95), say sources.

From TDS mismatches to capital gains errors: 3 risks under Income Tax Act 2025The Income Tax Act, 2025 is now in force, and for many companies, the transition seems smooth so far. But could small gaps like TDS mismatches and capital gains errors turn into bigger compliance issues?

Rupee sinks to record low as oil spike and Fed stance pile on pressureThe rupee slipped to a record low against the US dollar as crude oil prices surged and the Federal Reserve stayed hawkish. The move underlines pressure from India's rising import bill, firmer dollar demand and tighter global financial conditions.

Sensex down 400 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The BSE Sensex was down 1,215.91 points, or 1.54%, at 76,280.45 around 10:58 am, while the Nifty 50 fell 366.70 points, or 1.51%, to 23,811.25.
Sensex opens 700 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IndiGo down 2%The &P BSE Sensex was down 947.08 points to 76,549.28, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 317.60 points to 23,860.05 as of 10:01 am.

JPMorgan asked Vis Raghavan to go, Citi snapped him up for $52 million in 2 daysVis Raghavan moved from JPMorgan Chase to Citigroup's top investment banking role over a single weekend in February 2024. The rapid switch, later detailed by the Financial Times, sharpened attention on his exit and his rising clout at Citi.

Infosys won't cut jobs despite AI shift, says CEO Salil ParekhInfosys CEO Salil Parekh said the company does not plan layoffs despite the AI shift. The stance sets it apart from peers and underscores a hiring-and-reskilling push.

Crude oil tops $120, highest since 2022: What it means for markets and rupeeDisruptions around the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil transit route, have added to the uncertainty, taking crude oil prices to their highest levels since mid-2022.

8th Pay Commission update: Has the last date been extended amid employee demands?The Commission is holding a three-day consultation session from April 28 to April 30. During these meetings, representatives from various employee groups are discussing key issues related to pay, allowances and service conditions.

entertainment.headline: Actor Evelyn Sharma, husband Tushaan Bhindi split; to co-parent children Actor Evelyn Sharma and dental surgeon Tushaan Bhindi have decided to part ways. They say the split is amicable, and their focus remains on co-parenting their two children.

Salman Khan's superhero film with Raj and DK: See when he's set to start shoot Salman Khan is set to team up with Raj and DK for a superhero film planned to begin in October 2026. The film marks his first full-fledged superhero venture, while the makers are still finalising its female lead.

Salman Khan's cameo in Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji sparks online frenzy. Watch Salman Khan appears as Jiva Mahala in Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji, and his cameo has gone viral online. The brief sword-wielding sequence and standout dialogue have become an early talking point as the film opens in cinemas.

Ek Din review: Sai-Junaid's film is scenic, sweet and strangely lifelessRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026 Ek Din review: Sai Pallavi and Junaid Khan's romantic film follows Dinesh and Meera through a one-day love story shaped by memory loss. The film's striking Japan backdrop cannot compensate for thin writing and a romance that never convinces.

Meryl Streep, Matt Damon celebrate Emily Blunt, Stanley Tucci's Walk of Fame honour Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci received stars on the Hollywood Walk of Fame on Thursday. Robert Downey Jr, Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson, Matt Damon and Meryl Streep also celebrated their enduring screen legacy.

Where did the plot go wrong for Rajeev Khandelwal? Rajeev Khandelwal has returned to TV as the host of Tum Ho Naa - Ghar Ki Superstar, a reality show that doesn't seem to do justice to his stature as a small screen superstar. The move brings back questions about why a widely praised actor never managed to convert acclaim into lasting commercial momentum.

Pregnant Deepika shoots King with SRK, fans call leaked pics pure Renaissance magic Leaked pictures from King's South African schedule showed Deepika Padukone and Shah Rukh Khan walking hand in hand on set. The visuals sparked strong fan reactions as Deepika continued filming after announcing her second pregnancy.

Evangeline Lilly slams Disney, says AI is replacing artists who built the MCU Ant Man's Evangeline Lilly criticised Disney's latest layoffs after Marvel artist Andy Park said he had been let go. She said the cuts sidelined the artists who shaped the MCU and raised fears about AI replacing creative work.

Michael, Elvis, Bohemian Rhapsody: Three musical lives, one cinematic impact With Michael now in theatres, comparisons with Elvis and Bohemian Rhapsody are inevitable—different in style yet united in resonance, as all three continue to strike a chord with music lovers across audiences

Met Gala 2026 Indian guests: KJo to Deepika and Priyanka. When and where to watch Ahead of Met Gala 2026 on May 4, curiosity is building around the line-up of Indian celebrities. Apart from Karan Johar, Deepika Padukone, Ranveer Singh, Priyanka Chopra Jonas, Alia Bhatt and several others are expected to attend the global fashion event.

BTS ARIRANG comeback fuels HYBE's record-breaking quarter. Details HYBE posted its highest-ever first-quarter revenue after BTS returned with ARIRANG. The surge spread across touring, merchandise and Weverse, showing the strength of its fan ecosystem.

Bombay High Court dismisses PIL against Raja Shivaji title, no stay on film release The Bombay High Court refused to stay the release of Raja Shivaji over objections to its title. The bench said the late PIL was motivated and found nothing derogatory in the film's name.

Karan Johar to debut at Met Gala 2026? Here's what we know Karan Johar is reportedly set to attend the Met Gala 2026, with official confirmation still awaited. His expected debut comes as Indian names continue to gain visibility at the global fashion event.

The Devil Wears Prada 2 review: Perfectly polished. That's allRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026 The Devil Wears Prada 2 review: Nearly two decades later, Miranda Priestly returns as Runway fights for relevance in a digital-first media world. The sequel uses that struggle to examine how fashion, journalism and influence now bend before metrics.

Priyanka, Katrina or Nayanthara: Are Tumbbad 2 makers eyeing an A-list name? Tumbbad 2 is exploring a leading female star for a pivotal role as work on the sequel moves ahead. The talks add fresh intrigue to the film, which is set for December 2027 theatrical release.

Office Romance trailer: Jennifer Lopez leads a risky love story with Brett Goldstein The trailer for Office Romance introduces Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein as colleagues drawn to each other at work. It sets up a choice between following their feelings and protecting the company Jackie has built.

Pavitra Punia shuts down trolls over Mouni Roy surgery claims Pavitra Punia has defended Mouni Roy and lashed out at trolls over surgery speculation. In a video on social media, she said personal choices should be respected and accused suggestive captions of fuelling needless chatter.

Kareena reacts after Karisma's children get relief in Sunjay Kapur's asset case Kareena Kapoor extended support to her sister Karisma by sharing a hopeful message after the Delhi High Court ordered status quo on Sunjay Kapur's estate. The ruling preserves the assets during the will dispute and places Priya Kapur's claims under closer scrutiny.

Pagglait 2 confirmed: Sanya Malhotra returns, shoot to begin in August Pagglait 2 has been greenlit, with Sanya Malhotra and director Umesh Bist returning. The sequel adds another follow-up title to Netflix India's slate.

general news.headline: Pawan Khera gets pre-arrest bail in defamation case filed by Assam CM's wifeGranting relief to Congress leader Pawan Khera, the Supreme Court observed that the personal liberty of an individual, as enshrined under Article 21 of the Constitution, cannot be put in peril.

Blow-by-blow account: How TMC dharna over 'EVM manipulation' ended in anti-climaxA late-night row erupted over alleged EVM tampering after the Trinamool Congress claimed suspicious movement at a Kolkata strongroom. Election officials said the activity was part of routine postal ballot segregation even as as Mamata Banerjee asked party workers to maintain round-the-clock vigil outside strongrooms.

Sex harassment claims against JPMorgan exec fabricated by staffer? Report adds twistA former JPMorgan employee identified as Chirayu Rana has accused executive director Lorna Hajdini of sexual abuse in a lawsuit, while the bank says its internal inquiry found no evidence. The dispute has widened into claims of retaliation, with the case drawing scrutiny because the complainant and executive worked as colleagues rather than in a direct reporting line.

Jabalpur tragedy: Woman, son found tied to same life jacket, minister breaks downRescuers at Jabalpur boat tragedy site recovered the bodies of a mother and her young son, still locked in their final embrace.

Superparent CBSE: Education Board takes on new role, wants to parent India's parentsCBSE may call it a partnership, but for many parents, this feels like something new. A system that doesn't just teach children, but quietly begins to teach parents how to raise them.

Who is Lorna Hajdini, the JPMorgan exec accused of using junior as 'sex slave'A former male employee has accused JPMorgan executive Lorna Hajdini of sexual abuse, coercion and racial harassment in a bombshell lawsuit. Hajdini has denied the allegations. However, this case has put spotlight on the 37-year-old Hajdini, who has been working with JPMorgan for 15 years.

We raised alarm, crew paid no heed: Jabalpur boat tragedy survivor recounts horrorA boat carrying 30 people capsized at Bargi Dam in Jabalpur amid sudden strong winds, killing nine and leaving several missing, despite an earlier weather alert warning of hazardous conditions.

Rich Dad Poor Dad author exposes Pak's oil shock vs India's remarkable stabilityAcross the border, the picture looked very different. Despite the same global oil shock and crude prices climbing above $120 per barrel, petrol and diesel rates in India stayed largely unchanged across major cities.

How Socotra island of Yemen became Sikotar Mata in GujaratAncient Indian mariners worshipped a Yemeni island and brought it back home as a deity. The island of Socotra, near East Africa, is believed to have manifested as Sikotar Mata, a sea goddess worshipped in Gujarat even today. Socotra was a vital refuge in the Arabian Sea for maritime traders, over 2,000 km from home.

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

Remember Parag Agrawal? His new company is now worth Rs 19,020 croreFormer Twitter (now X) CEO Parag Agrawal is back in the news. Parag who was fired by Elon Musk, soon after he bought Twitter in 2022, has raised $100 million for his new AI startup called Parallel Web Systems, taking its valuation to around Rs 19,020 crore.

'People do not want future of humanity in Musk's hands', Judge remarks in OpenAI trialJudge Gonzalez Rogers pushed Elon Musk's legal team away from existential-risk arguments as his cross-examination in the OpenAI case intensified. The exchanges sharpened scrutiny of Musk's credibility, xAI's training practices and the high financial stakes of the lawsuit.

Rude, obsessed with colonialism: Mamdani slammed over 'return the Kohinoor' remarkZohran Mamdani said he would encourage King Charles III to return the Kohinoor before meeting him at the 9/11 Memorial. The remark triggered criticism over his handling of the royal visit, while officials gave no details of their brief exchange.

LPG Price Rise: Commercial gas cylinder prices up. Check city-wise ratesLPG price hike from May 1, 2026 raised commercial gas cylinder rates by Rs 993 across India. Check updated LPG prices in Delhi, Mumbai and Bengaluru, while domestic cylinder prices remained unchanged.

Even the bank didn't help me: What victims face after reporting digital fraudFor part two of our series on Banking Risks in Digital India, I spoke to victims of digital fraud to understand what they experienced after being scammed. Their stories show how the aftermath can be just as distressing as the fraud itself.

The film's striking Japan backdrop cannot compensate for thin writing and a romance that never convinces.

Odisha bank at fault for not helping man who carried sister's skeleton: ProbeAn Odisha government preliminary probe found bank staff in Keonjhar may not have cooperated with the man who carried his sister's skeleton to access her savings. The findings point to possible procedural lapses and prompted warnings from authorities for strict action.

Where exactly is leftover of the Left in India?From ruling three states at a time to surviving in scattered pockets, India's Communist parties are facing an existential crisis. Exit polls suggest the Left front could lose Kerala, the only state it governs now. The question is not if the Left exists, but where it exists in India.
Masala dosa to malpua, 11 Indian dishes crack World's Top 100 pancakesFood guide TasteAtlas has featured 11 Indian dishes from across India in its April Top 100 Pancakes list.

Semaglutide trial shows sharp drop in heavy drinking daysA study published in The Lancet reports that weekly semaglutide injections reduced heavy drinking in adults with obesity and alcohol use disorders. The findings add to growing interest in GLP-1 drugs beyond weight loss, though the study was small.

Summer intensifies, heat to return: When will it rain again?As a result of rain and winds, pleasant nights were felt across most of North India, easing the recent concerns of rising nighttime temperatures that usually help cool down places and people alike.

The Devil Wears Prada 2 review: Perfectly polished. That's allRelease Date:Fri May 01, 2026The Devil Wears Prada 2 review: Nearly two decades later, Miranda Priestly returns as Runway fights for relevance in a digital-first media world. The sequel uses that struggle to examine how fashion, journalism and influence now bend before metrics.

White House event shooter seen storming security, opening fire in new CCTV footageSecurity camera footage released by Jeanine Pirro shows Cole Tomas Allen running through a checkpoint and shooting a federal agent at the Washington Hilton during the White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

sports.headline: Will Heinrich Klaasen make South Africa return? SRH star reacts to Pietersen remark Heinrich Klaasen has ruled out a return to international cricket despite calls for a comeback. The South African says his focus is on franchise cricket, Rotterdam in the ETPL and staying mentally fresh.

Trump backs Iran's 2026 World Cup entry in USA after FIFA president's confirmation Donald Trump said he was fine with Iran playing their 2026 World Cup matches in the United States after Gianni Infantino confirmed FIFA's position. The decision keeps a politically charged schedule intact despite Tehran's venue concerns and its delegation's absence from FIFA Congress.

Not young anymore: Bhuvneshwar Kumar plays down Purple Cap milestone focuses on team IPL 2026: Bhuvneshwar Kumar returned with figures of 3/28 against GT, taking his tally to 17 wickets from nine matches and regaining the Purple Cap as the tournament's leading wicket-taker.

After CSK jibe, Shubman Gill makes play bold dig at RCB with Virat Kohli picture Shubman Gill followed Gujarat Titans' win over Royal Challengers Bengaluru with an Instagram post praising Jason Holder and appearing to needle RCB. The post extended the night's playful edge, which also featured banter with Virat Kohli before the two embraced after the match.

RR vs DC Preview: Time for Riyan Parag to step up amidst myriad of controversies RR vs DC Preview: Riyan Parag faces mounting pressure in Jaipur as Rajasthan Royals seek stability, while Delhi Capitals arrive searching for inspiration, clarity and a much-needed spark.

How has Holder filled the Hardik Pandya void for GT? Vikram Solanki explains Jason Holder's all-round display against Royal Challengers Bengaluru powered Gujarat Titans' win and earned warm praise from Vikram Solanki. As Gujarat push for the playoffs, Holder provides balance, experience and communication to fill the gap left by Hardik Pandya's exit in 2024.

IPL Play of the Day: Kohli, Rabada go blow for blow in Ahmedabad's main event GT vs RCB, IPL Play of the Day: Virat Kohli and Kagiso Rabada traded early blows in Ahmedabad, with the RCB star's powerplay burst met by the GT pacer's sharp tactical adjustment and decisive comeback.

Can India outclass Taipei? All you need to know about Thomas Cup quarters Thomas Cup 2026 quarterfinals: India will head into the last-eight tie against Chinese Taipei in Denmark with a slender edge. Lakshya Sen, Ayush Shetty and Kidambi Srikanth will play the singles matches for India on Friday. Here's all you need to know about the big quarterfinal tie.

Riyan Parag vaping row a reminder players need to maintain culture: Sangakkara Kumar Sangakkara said Rajasthan Royals' recent controversies have been addressed internally, calling them a reminder for players to uphold team culture ahead of their clash against Delhi Capitals.

Gujarat scratching their heads over middle order woes? Rashid Khan opens up IPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Rashid Khan said Gujarat Titans should not overthink their inconsistent middle order, stressing that T20 cricket exposes every team's weaknesses. On Thursday, Rashid took two wickets as GT beat RCB by four wickets in Ahmedabad.

How fearless Shubman Gill sucked the venom out of RCB pace attack IPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Jos Buttler revealed that Shubman Gill planned a bold counterattack against Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood, as Gujarat Titans beat Royal Challengers Bengaluru by 4 wickets on Thursday in Ahmedabad.

Gujarat Titans take sweet revenge as Bengaluru endure rare off dayIPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Gujarat Titans beat the Royal Challengers Bengaluru by four wickets at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Chasing 156, Shubman Gill's men romped home in 15.5 overs.

Virat Kohli gives Shubman Gill death stare, fiery send-off in Ahmedabad: WatchGT vs RCB, IPL 2026: Virat Kohli brought the intensity with an animated send-off to Shubman Gill, but Gujarat Titans stayed on top after Kagiso Rabada and Jason Holder dented RCB in Ahmedabad.

Virat Kohli hits out at umpires after Jason Holder's catch to dismiss Rajat PatidarGT vs RCB, IPL 2026: Virat Kohli was not happy after Rajat Patidar's controversial dismissal against Gujarat Titans, with replays raising questions over Jason Holder's catch and the third umpire's review.

Virat Kohli dominates Kagiso Rabada with boundary blitz, GT pacer answers with revengeIPL 2026, GT vs RCB: Virat Kohli dominated Kagiso Rabada early with a stunning burst of five consecutive boundaries in IPL 2026, but the GT pacer fought back strongly. Rabada had the last laugh by dismissing Kohli for 28.

Was Riyan Parag let off after vaping? Fans call out hypocrisy after escaping banRiyan Parag's dressing-room vaping incident has sparked backlash after the IPL fined him 20 per cent of his match fee, with fans questioning whether the Rajasthan Royals captain was punished lightly.

MCC defends umpires in Angkrish Raghuvanshi Obstructing the Field dismissal callThe MCC defended the umpire's decision to give Angkrish Raghuvanshi out obstructing the field against Lucknow Super Giants, stating it aligns with long-standing laws that penalise wilful changes of running direction.

Indonesia's Thomas Cup exit, a historic first in 68 yearsIndonesia's shock Thomas Cup group-stage exit ends a 68-year run of dominance, exposing a rare crack in badminton's most enduring empire and possibly marking a defining shift in the sport's power balance.

Lungi Ngidi returns to bowling for Delhi capitals after recovering from head injuryIPL 2026: Lungi Ngidi has returned to Delhi Capitals training after the head injury he suffered against Punjab Kings. The footage offers relief, but concussion protocol could still keep him out of the Rajasthan Royals match.

GT vs RCB Highlights: Gujarat beat Bengaluru by 4 wickets in AhmedabadGujarat (GT) vs Bengaluru (RCB) Highlights, IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans earned two crucial points after beating the Royal Challengers Bengaluru by four wickets on Thursday at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Jensi Kanabar, 14, rewrites history books, surpasses Sania Mirza's all-time recordJensi Kanabar became the youngest Indian female to reach a professional singles quarter-final at 14 years and five months, surpassing Sania Mirza's long-standing record with a composed run at the ITF W15 New Delhi.

Cristiano Ronaldo five UCL gesture goes viral after Al Ahli fans' title jibeCristiano Ronaldo answered Al Ahli fans by holding up five fingers after Al Nassr's 2-0 win. The exchange went viral and sharpened the bad blood around a match already clouded by refereeing complaints and on-pitch tension.

Release Hardik Pandya: Mumbai Indians told to reset after 3 mellow years in IPLSimon Doull has urged Mumbai Indians to consider releasing Hardik Pandya if they don't think he will be able to lead the team into the next cycle. Mumbai Indians have had a tough time in IPL 2026. They are rooted to the 9th spot with just two wins in eight games.

Fresh trouble for Riyan Parag? RR captain escapes ban, but women's group seeks probeA women's group has sought an inquiry into visuals purportedly showing Riyan Parag with a vape-like device during an IPL match. It says the incident could normalise a banned product among young viewers and wants accountability.

Riyan Parag escapes ban for vaping during IPL match, fined by BCCIIPL 2026: The Board of Control for Cricket in India has fined Riyan Parag 25% of his match fee for vaping during an IPL match in Mullanpur, with the Rajasthan Royals captain escaping a stricter punishment.

Watch: Venkatesh Prasad's child-like joy in Bengaluru hailstorm, tastes it tooFormer India cricketer Venkatesh Prasad stopped his car during Bengaluru's sudden hailstorm and shared a playful video online. The clip struck a chord as the rare spell cooled the city and briefly turned parts of it white.

IPL business first, cricket next? Sri Lanka great's massive claim on run-festsIPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad's spin bowling coach Muttiah Muralitharan said flat wickets and the IPL's design are fuelling relentless 200-plus totals. He argued the league prioritises entertainment, sponsors and viewer interest over cricket development after SRH's victory over MI with eight balls to spare.

Travishek Blueprint: The simple secret behind IPL's most destructive opening pairIPL 2026: Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma powered Sunrisers Hyderabad's chase of 245 against Mumbai Indians with another explosive opening stand. Their contrasting strengths and uncomplicated method continue to drive SRH's winning run.

Call Klaasen and add him to 2027 World Cup squad, Pietersen tells South AfricaIPL 2026: Kevin Pietersen has urged Cricket South Africa to convince Heinrich Klaasen to reverse his retirement, citing his match-winning form for Sunrisers Hyderabad. Klaasen, has been in a sensational form in the IPL scoring 414 runs at a strike-rate of 15741, placing him second in the Orange Cap race behind teammate Abhishek Sharma.

Gujarat Titans hope to erase mistakes of the past vs red-hot RCBGT vs RCB, IPL 2026 preview: Gujarat Titans are set to face Royal Challengers Bengaluru in their reverse fixture on Thursday. GT need to come out of their shell and adapt the modern T20 template if they want to beat a red-hot RCB.

Champions League: Arteta cries foul over VAR call as Arsenal held by AtleticoChampions League semi-final first leg: Coach Mikel Arteta hit out after Arsenal's penalty was overturned by VAR in Madrid. He said the decision denied a potentially decisive win.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
